name: Republic of the Congo
experiences: Brazzaville's Congo River corniche, Basilique Sainte-Anne du Congo, and the Pierre Savorgnan de Brazza Memorial
Odzala-Kokoua rainforest stays split between Ngaga, Mboko, and Lango with western lowland gorilla tracking
Pointe-Noire's Atlantic coast, Gorges de Diosso viewpoints, and seafood-heavy dining scene
Loango Bay and the former Loango Kingdom coast for beach downtime, Ma-Loango heritage, and pre-arranged small-boat outings
avoid: October to May is much wetter, so rainforest trails, Diosso road conditions, and beach plans around Pointe-Noire are less predictable than in the June to September dry season.
Odzala-Kokoua is an expensive logistics-heavy add-on: gorilla trekking normally works through all-inclusive lodge stays and charter transfers rather than spontaneous walk-up travel.
French is the working language for taxis, hotels, and park logistics, and cash in XAF still matters for church donations, taxi bargaining, and small attraction tickets.
stopoverNote: Use Brazzaville as the planning base, carve out Odzala as a dedicated fly-in safari block, then return south for the easier Brazzaville-Pointe-Noire domestic hop on ECAir or Trans Air Congo before finishing on the Loango Bay coast.

cities.name: Brazzaville
cities.notes: Basilique Sainte-Anne du Congo, the Brazza Memorial, Congo River viewpoints, strong hotel base, and the easiest place to set up flights and park logistics.

cities.name: Odzala-Kokoua National Park
cities.notes: Ngaga, Mboko, and Lango camp circuit with western lowland gorilla tracking, baï wildlife walks, and mokoro or motor-boat moves on the Lekoli system.

cities.name: Pointe-Noire
cities.notes: Republic of the Congo's coastal hub for the Gorges de Diosso, Côte Sauvage beaches, seafood restaurants, and easy taxi links north toward Loango Bay.

cities.name: Loango Bay
cities.notes: Quiet Atlantic shoreline north of Pointe-Noire with Malonda Lodge stays, Ma-Loango heritage visits, and small-boat or fishing departures arranged in advance.

itinerary.sim: Airtel Congo and MTN Congo are the easiest options for this route; budget roughly ₹700–₹1,800 for a starter data bundle, but expect weaker data once you move into Odzala where the camps rely more on lodge Wi-Fi and satellite-backed communications.

connections.method: Most travelers go in on lodge-arranged charter flights and camp transfers rather than public transport, so treat Odzala as a pre-booked safari block instead of a DIY side trip.
Return by charter to Brazzaville first, then switch to the scheduled Brazzaville-Pointe-Noire domestic hop on ECAir or Trans Air Congo for the most reliable southbound move.
connections.cost: ₹9,800–₹12,500 plus the included Odzala air transfer

connections.method: Private taxis are simplest for the short coastal transfer north; ask the hotel to set the fare in advance if you are heading directly to Malonda or Côte Sauvage.
The return is the same quick taxi run, though some lodges can also arrange a fishing boat or coastal outing before dropping you back into town.

extras: June to September is the best practical window: Brazzaville is less humid, Odzala tracking is easier underfoot, and Pointe-Noire plus Loango Bay are far more reliable for beach days.

Basilique Sainte-Anne du Congo is usually donation-based, while the Brazza Memorial and Gorges de Diosso use small cash tickets, so keep low-denomination XAF on hand even when staying at premium hotels.
Odzala pricing looks extreme because the charter flights, guiding, conservation fees, meals, and remote luxury-camp logistics are typically packaged together rather than sold à la carte.
French goes a long way with taxi drivers in both Brazzaville and Pointe-Noire; having hotel staff write down destinations helps, especially for Diosso and Loango runs.
Trans Air Congo and ECAir are the domestic names worth checking first for the Brazzaville-Pointe-Noire sector, but schedules can shift, so build a little flex into the handoff after Odzala.

Loango Bay: Loango Bay Republic of the Congo
